_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with gastric biopsy and H. pylori culture/susceptibility
procedure.indication: Treatment-refractory Helicobacter pylori infection with persistent dyspepsia; gastric biopsy for culture/susceptibility to guide next-line eradication
procedure.sedation: Moderate sedation (midazolam 2 mg, fentanyl 75 mcg) with topical pharyngeal anesthesia
procedure.prep_quality: NPO >8 hours; adequate mucosal visualization. Pantoprazole held 2 weeks pre-procedure per plan.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with gastric biopsies. INDICATION: refractory H. pylori infection, biopsy for culture and susceptibility. SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl). The endoscope was passed under direct vision to the second portion of the duodenum. ESOPHAGUS: normal mucosa; Z-line at 40 cm, no esophagitis, no Barrett's, no stricture or mass. STOMACH: mild-to-moderate erythematous, non-erosive antral gastritis with a nodular appearance of the antral mucosa consistent with H. pylori-associated changes. No gastric ulcer, no mass, no bleeding, no stigmata of recent hemorrhage. Rugal folds normal. Retroflexion of the cardia and fundus unremarkable. DUODENUM: normal bulb and D2, no ulceration. Targeted biopsies obtained per Sydney protocol (antrum x2, incisura x1, body x2) — a subset submitted fresh for H. pylori culture and antimicrobial susceptibility, the remainder in formalin for histology. Rapid urease test (CLO) performed intra-procedurally. IMPRESSION: Nodular non-erosive antral gastritis consistent with persistent H. pylori infection; no ulcer, mass, or bleeding source. Gastric biopsies sent for histology and culture/susceptibility.

findings.finding: Nodular non-erosive antral gastritis with mucosal erythema, consistent with H. pylori
findings.action_taken: biopsies obtained (Sydney protocol); subset for culture/susceptibility

findings.finding: Normal duodenal bulb and D2, no ulceration
No ulcer, mass, or bleeding source

pathology.report: Rapid urease (CLO) test POSITIVE intra-procedurally. Histology and H. pylori culture with antimicrobial susceptibility pending (culture typically 10-14 days).

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated procedure well. Instructed to remain off antibiotics until susceptibility-guided regimen is selected; may resume symptom control per clinic instructions.

followup.recommendation: Review gastric biopsy histology and H. pylori culture/susceptibility results; call patient and select culture-guided salvage eradication regimen. AVOID amoxicillin-based regimens given penicillin allergy (itchy rash).
Prescribe next-line H. pylori eradication therapy guided by susceptibility and penicillin allergy; confirm patient is off all antibiotics >=4 weeks before any test-of-cure
Test-of-cure (stool antigen or urea breath test) >=4 weeks after completing eradication therapy and >=2 weeks off PPI
Resume/maintain pantoprazole for dyspepsia symptom control now that biopsies are obtained; hold PPI again 2 weeks before any test-of-cure

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric biopsy H. pylori culture and antimicrobial susceptibility pending (10-14 days) — the entire purpose of the EGD; result must drive selection of the salvage eradication regimen
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review culture/susceptibility, call patient, prescribe susceptibility-guided regimen

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric biopsy histology pending (Sydney protocol) — assess gastritis severity, intestinal metaplasia/atrophy, exclude MALT/dysplasia
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review histology and document; escalate if atrophy/IM/dysplasia found

planted_followup_items.description: Allergy-constrained salvage antibiotic selection — penicillin/amoxicillin allergy (rash) newly identified; must avoid amoxicillin-based regimens; ID co-management referral in place
planted_followup_items.expected_action: select non-amoxicillin regimen with GI/ID per susceptibility

planted_followup_items.description: Test-of-cure required after salvage eradication (>=4 weeks post-therapy, >=2 weeks off PPI) — non-invasive stool antigen or urea breath test; repeat EGD only if therapy again fails or alarm features develop
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule non-invasive test-of-cure and confirm eradication

planted_followup_items.description: Pantoprazole management — PPI was held 2 weeks pre-procedure for biopsy accuracy (not an anticoagulant hold); resume for symptom control now that biopsies are obtained, then re-hold 2 weeks before any test-of-cure to avoid false-negative
planted_followup_items.expected_action: instruct patient on PPI resume/hold timing around test-of-cure

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I've been kind of burpy and bloated since the camera test. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation bloating/belching after a diagnostic scope with biopsies
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after EGD and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges; call for fever, worsening abdominal or chest pain, black stools, or vomiting blood.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms after a diagnostic EGD; no complication, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will you know about the stomach biopsies, and what antibiotic will I get since I'm allergic to penicillin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending H. pylori culture/susceptibility timing and allergy-constrained salvage regimen
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: culture/susceptibility takes about 10-14 days (expected ~2026-08-18); GI will call with results and choose a non-amoxicillin, susceptibility-guided regimen given the penicillin allergy. Stay off antibiotics until then.
contacts.rationale: Answerable directly from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending culture/susceptibility item (F1) and the documented penicillin allergy.

contacts.verbatim: Can I start taking my pantoprazole again for the burning, or am I supposed to keep it stopped?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about PPI resumption timing after the biopsy hold
contacts.expected_action: Answer: pantoprazole may be resumed now for symptom control since the biopsies are already obtained; it will need to be held again for 2 weeks before any future test-of-cure. Use antacid/H2 as needed in the meantime.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the medication-reconciliation follow-up (F5); PPI was held only for biopsy accuracy and can safely resume post-procedure.
